title: Item View Name

posts.author: Adnene (Adnene)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey guys,

We are working on a new Item View feature and wondering how we can fetch the item view tab name (like board view name in a board view) ?

With a board view we used to call the graphQL API providing boardId and boardViewId which give us the name of the view set by user.

const response = await monday.api(`query get_views{
      boards(ids: [${context.boardId}]) {
          views(ids: [${context.boardViewId}]) {
              id
              name
          }
      }
    }`);

in the case of an ItemView, we see when calling monday.get('context') that we have boardId and instanceId but no way to get the name given by the user to the newly added tab.

We looked already in the API schema, documentation, articles to find a way to grab that name without success.

Does any one had a similar use case and know how we can do that ?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @Adnene and welcome to the community!

I hope you like it here

As of today there is no way to get the name of the tab of the item view via SDK or API.

Let me know if you have any other questions

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, @Matias.Monday . It would be great if the API is able to support getting the name of the tab in the item view so that it’s on par with the same feature currently available for board views. This allows us to display integration recipes in a user-friendly way, rather than displaying view IDs.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @samicaracand,

I have added your vote for this request!

Cheers,
Matias
